requirements.text: WHEN a user opens Documents View, the system shall show configured document root folders collapsed by default.
WHEN a user opens a selected document from a route or file selection, the system shall expand that document's ancestor folders and keep the document highlighted.
WHEN a user activates the collapse-all control, the system shall collapse document folders while leaving selected-document ancestors expanded.
WHEN a user opens documents in a project, the system shall show up to five project-scoped recent document shortcuts.
WHEN a user types in the document tree filter, the system shall filter visible documents by file name, title, and project-relative path using all query terms.
WHEN the selected document is hidden by the active filter and the user targets the active document, the system shall clear the filter, expand ancestors, and scroll the selected row into view.
WHEN a broad document root includes docs/CRs, the system shall omit docs/CRs from Documents View navigation.
WHEN path configuration includes a broad root that overlaps docs/CRs, the system shall communicate that docs/CRs is automatically excluded.
The system shall keep Documents sidebar icon controls accessible by keyboard and labelled for assistive technology.
The system shall keep the sidebar filter as tree filtering and shall not perform full-text document content search.
WHEN Documents View loads, the system shall not show unmanaged pinned or scoped shortcut lists.
The system shall treat docs/CRs as ticket territory and never persist it as a recent document shortcut.
IF a recent document becomes deleted or excluded, THEN the system shall remove that shortcut without breaking Documents View.
WHEN recent document shortcuts are visible, the system shall let the user collapse and expand the Recent section without changing the selected document.
WHEN recent document shortcuts are shown, the system shall render each shortcut with the same title and filename treatment as file rows in the tree.
WHEN the document tree is long, the Recent section shall remain fixed above the independently scrollable tree block.

scenarios.title: Document roots start collapsed while active path is visible
scenarios.given: I have a project with nested configured document folders and an active document route
scenarios.then: I see root folders collapsed except the active document's ancestor path, and the active document row is highlighted

scenarios.title: Collapse all keeps the active document recoverable
scenarios.given: I am viewing a selected document in an expanded document tree
scenarios.then: The tree collapses unrelated folders while the selected document's ancestor path stays expanded

scenarios.title: Recent documents provide project-scoped shortcuts
scenarios.given: I have opened more than five documents in one project
scenarios.then: I see no more than five recent document shortcuts for that project

scenarios.title: Tree filter matches title, filename, and path
scenarios.given: I am viewing configured documents with titles, filenames, and nested paths
scenarios.when: I type multiple words into the document filter
scenarios.then: The tree shows only documents whose title, filename, or project-relative path matches all query terms

scenarios.title: Target action recovers active document hidden by filter
scenarios.given: I have a selected document hidden by the active tree filter
scenarios.when: I activate the target active document control
scenarios.then: The filter clears, ancestor folders expand, and the selected document row scrolls into view

scenarios.title: Path configuration discloses ticket-area exclusion
scenarios.given: I configure docs as a document root
scenarios.when: I review document path configuration or open Documents View
scenarios.then: The interface communicates that docs/CRs is excluded and the Documents tree omits docs/CRs

scenarios.title: Documents navigation avoids unmanaged shortcuts
scenarios.given: I am in Documents View with a large configured document tree
scenarios.then: No pinned root or scoped shortcut list is shown above the tree

scenarios.title: Recent documents collapse and expand
scenarios.given: Recent document shortcuts are visible in Documents View
scenarios.when: I collapse and expand the Recent section
scenarios.then: The shortcut rows hide and return without changing the document tree

scenarios.title: Recent document rows match tree file rows
scenarios.given: A recent document has title and filename metadata in the document tree
scenarios.when: The Recent section renders that shortcut
scenarios.then: The shortcut shows the title as primary text and filename as secondary text using the tree file row treatment

scenarios.title: Recent remains fixed while tree scrolls
scenarios.given: Recent documents are visible above a long document tree
scenarios.then: Recent stays in place and only the tree content scrolls

obligations.title: FileTree owns collapsed root state and selected ancestor expansion
DocumentsLayout owns the collapse-all control and delegates tree state changes through FileTree handle
DocumentsLayout owns tree filtering and active-document recovery behavior
TreeService filters ticket territory from document discovery and PathSelector discloses automatic exclusion
DocumentsLayout avoids unmanaged pinned and scoped shortcut UI
Document navigation controls expose stable data-testid selectors for Playwright coverage
RecentDocuments and documentNavigation config own project-scoped recent file shortcuts and section collapse

testPlans.title: FileTree collapsed defaults and selected ancestor expansion
Ticket area is excluded from Documents navigation

tasks.title: Exclude ticket territory from document discovery and path configuration
Update FileTree collapsed state and selected ancestor behavior
Verify design docs and end-to-end navigation contract
Add sidebar recents, filter, and target recovery
